intro: Ochetophila nana, also known as Dwarf Mountain Mahogany, is a small shrub native to the high-altitude regions of the Andes mountain range. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ochetophila nana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ochetophila nana is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are considered to be effective remedies for respiratory issues and other health conditions.
